title: Ligolo-ng TUN Pivot and Route Injection
description: This runbook operationalizes Ligolo-ng TUN Pivot and Route Injection using #ip . It is designed for repeatable, high-signal execution in authorized OSCP/CPTS-style assessments. Creates tun-based pivoting without SOCKS limitations for deeper internal operations.
shortDescription: Ligolo-ng TUN Pivot and Route Injection

metadata.descriptionHtml:
This runbook operationalizes Ligolo-ng TUN Pivot and Route Injection using #ip. It is designed for repeatable, high-signal execution in authorized OSCP/CPTS-style assessments.

Creates tun-based pivoting without SOCKS limitations for deeper internal operations.

  1. Prepare scope and access: validate target reachability and select low-noise execution order.
  2. Execute core workflow: run the command chain to produce actionable security outcomes.
  3. Validate and operationalize output: confirm results, capture artifacts, and prepare follow-on actions.
  • Tool Location/Source: primary references are in local source corpus entries linked from runbook-list.md; e.g., docs/sources-runbooks/oscp-cpts/p0/dollarboysushil-oscp-cpts-notes/pivoting-and-tunneling/ligolo-ng.md:27; docs/sources-runbooks/oscp-cpts/p0/dollarboysushil-oscp-cpts-notes/pivoting-and-tunneling/ligolo-ng.md:29.
  • Attack pre-requisite: authorized scope, operator shell access, and protocol reachability on TCP 80, TCP 443.
  • What attacker gain: validated findings (access paths, credentials, execution context, or exposure evidence) enabling faster compromise progression.
  • Detection/Defense opsec: monitor abnormal auth/enumeration bursts, enforce least privilege and hardening baselines, and alert on repeated tool-pattern telemetry.

Core Commands:

sudo ip tuntap add user <user> mode tun ligolo
sudo ip link set ligolo up
sudo ip route add <INTERNAL_CIDR> dev ligolo
metadata.commandsBlock:

sudo ip link set ligolo up
